obligations.reason: The fact layer shows key_in copied to key_big and multiple read-mux assignments returning key_big slices and inter_state slices on external_bus_io.rdata. Verify whether these addresses are reachable from software-visible APB/MMIO and whether any privilege/authorization guard mediates those reads.
obligations.subject: software-visible APB/MMIO requester via aes_p* / external_bus_io
obligations.expected_guard: reads of AES key material and internal intermediate state should be blocked or require an explicit privileged/debug-authorized mode; normal MMIO/APB reads should expose only non-secret status/ciphertext as intended
obligations.candidate_path: APB/reg bus -> aes_wrapper.external_bus_io address decode -> read mux -> external_bus_io.rdata
state_conditions_to_check: external_bus_io read transaction active for key/inter_state offsets
reset/idle behavior of inter_state if relevant
required_evidence_slots: read operation/address decode for key/inter_state offsets
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect full aes_wrapper address decode and ariane_peripherals APB connection to confirm bus reachability and whether there are hidden guards not captured in assignments.

obligations.reason: The wrapper accepts external_bus_io.wdata into start, p_c, and state registers. Check whether the visible RTL enforces address, write-enable, and any privilege/lock guard before changing AES operation state.
obligations.subject: software-visible APB/MMIO requester
obligations.object: AES plaintext/control/state registers p_c, state, start
obligations.expected_guard: writes that configure cryptographic operation inputs/control should be constrained by valid address decode and any required privilege/lock policy for the AES peripheral; write strobes should not permit unintended update aliases
obligations.candidate_path: APB/reg bus -> aes_wrapper.external_bus_io write decode -> p_c/state/start registers -> aes_192_sed instance
obligations.uncertainty: Protected status of p_c/state depends on system policy; obligation is to verify guard adequacy rather than assume vulnerability.

obligations.reason: bootrom has req_i, we_i, addr_i, wdata_i ports and a direct rdata_o assignment from mem indexed by raddr_q. Check whether writes are impossible/ignored and whether reads are only reachable in the intended mapped range and subject context.
obligations.subject: AXI/reg requester connected through testharness boot ROM path
obligations.object: bootrom mem contents exposed through rdata_o
obligations.expected_guard: boot ROM contents should be read-only; write requests should be ignored or rejected, and read access should be limited to intended boot address range/requesters by upstream decode or local guard
obligations.candidate_path: ROM bus request -> bootrom.req_i/we_i/addr_i -> raddr_q -> mem[raddr_q] -> rdata_o
state_conditions_to_check: request not asserted but rdata_o observable
required_evidence_slots: local behavior for req_i and we_i
address range check or upstream decode
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows rdata_o bounds check but not complete req/we handling or upstream decode; inspect full module and harness connections.

obligations.reason: The peripheral facts include rom2 with req/we/address/data ports and ariane_peripherals signals pkey_loc_o, rom2_rdata, rom2_wdata. Verify whether key-location or ROM contents are exposed to unprivileged APB/MMIO without guard and whether writes can alter them.
obligations.subject: software-visible APB/MMIO requester in ariane_peripherals
obligations.object: rom2 memory/read data and pkey_loc_o/key-location related output
obligations.expected_guard: ROM2 contents and any key-location output should be read-only or access-controlled; writes should be rejected/ignored and reads of key-related locations should require intended privilege/authorization or be non-secret by design
obligations.candidate_path: APB/reg bus -> ariane_peripherals rom2 i_apb_to_reg -> rom2.req_i/we_i/addr_i -> rdata_o and pkey_loc_o
required_evidence_slots: rom2 address decode and read mux
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect rom2 internals and ariane_peripherals mappings to determine whether pkey_loc_o is sensitive and externally readable.

obligations.reason: The scope includes SimJTAG, dmi_jtag, SimDTM, debug_req*, dmi_req*, jtag_req*, debug_req_core, ndmreset, and AXI debug master/slave connection instances. Check whether debug-originated accesses are gated by authentication, jtag_key, test_en, or lifecycle authorization before reaching protected assets.
obligations.subject: JTAG/DMI debug requester
obligations.object: SoC memory/peripherals/core debug control reachable via debug_req/debug_resp and AXI debug master/slave
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG/DMI debug operations that can read/write memory or control the core should require debug authorization/authentication or an explicit test/debug enable lifecycle state, and must not bypass normal privilege/PMP for protected regions unless authorized
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DTM/DMI signals -> dmi_jtag/SimDTM -> debug request/response -> debug module AXI master/slave connections -> SoC memory/peripherals
required_evidence_slots: protected target path (core control or AXI memory/peripheral)
authorization guard signal and use site
obligations.uncertainty: Visible facts list key/debug signals but not their logic; must inspect harness to distinguish simulation-only wiring from security-relevant reachable path.

obligations.reason: axi_node_intf_wrap has test_en_i and the harness has test_en. Check whether test enable affects arbitration, connectivity mapping, reset behavior, or guards in a way that permits unauthorized master-to-subordinate access.
obligations.subject: test interface or logic controlling test_en
obligations.object: AXI routing/access-control behavior and protected subordinate ports
obligations.expected_guard: test enable should not disable access-control checks or alter routing to protected assets except in an authorized test lifecycle mode; any test bypass must be gated and not software/debug-controllable in normal operation
obligations.candidate_path: test_en_i/test_en -> axi_node_intf_wrap and reset/debug fabric paths
state_conditions_to_check: active AXI read/write transfer while test mode changes
required_evidence_slots: protected target reachable under test mode
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer does not show test_en_i assignments or use sites; inspect module body.

obligations.reason: The AXI wrapper exposes priv_lvl_i and connectivity_mapping plus direct channel assignments for AW/AR/W/R/B. Verify that privilege is actually used to gate routing for protected address ranges on both reads and writes, not merely propagated or ignored.
obligations.subject: AXI master/requester with privilege level
obligations.object: AXI subordinate address regions/peripheral windows behind axi_node_intf_wrap
obligations.expected_guard: routing to protected subordinates should enforce privilege/access matrix consistently for read and write channels using priv_lvl_i and/or AXI protection attributes; unprivileged accesses should not be routed or acknowledged as successful
obligations.candidate_path: AXI master interfaces -> axi_node_intf_wrap -> connectivity_mapping(priv_lvl_i, requester attributes) -> subordinate routing
required_evidence_slots: read and write route enable conditions
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect omitted/truncated assignments and connectivity_mapping logic to know actual policy.

obligations.reason: The peripheral module contains multiple APB-to-reg instances and separate paddr/psel/pwrite/prdata signals. Check that address decode and psel generation prevent an unauthorized or unintended requester from accessing sensitive windows or causing aliasing between windows.
obligations.subject: software-visible APB/MMIO requester
obligations.object: PLIC, packet, AES, UART, ROM2 APB-visible register windows
obligations.expected_guard: security-sensitive peripheral configuration registers should be reachable only through intended address windows and privilege policy; APB decode must not alias protected registers across peripherals or ignore psel/pwrite/penable
obligations.candidate_path: APB-to-reg bridges in ariane_peripherals -> PLIC/AES/PKT/UART register windows -> peripheral reg interfaces
obligations.uncertainty: Specific protected registers besides AES/ROM2 need classification during inspection.

obligations.reason: The harness includes ndmreset and reset generator, while AES wrapper contains secret/intermediate registers. Check whether resets from debug/test sources are authorized and whether sensitive state is cleared or left externally readable after reset transitions.
obligations.subject: debug/JTAG reset requester or external reset source
obligations.object: AES key/intermediate/control state, debug authorization state, ROM/peripheral security state
obligations.expected_guard: debug-triggered reset should not leave security-sensitive state uncleared or force a mode transition that bypasses locks/authorization; reset sources should be authorized and clear key/intermediate/debug state as required
obligations.candidate_path: debug reset signals ndmreset/ndmreset_n -> reset generator/core/peripheral resets -> protected state registers
state_conditions_to_check: AES operation in progress at reset
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect reset wiring and whether key_in is external persistent state not reset locally.
